obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way that they achieve an appropriate level of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity, and that they perform consistently in those respects throughout their lifecycle.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI system quality engineering, including accuracy measurement and benchmarking, robustness testing and assurance, cybersecurity integration for AI systems, and lifecycle performance consistency monitoring.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI system quality engineering, including accuracy measurement and benchmarking, robustness testing and assurance, cybersecurity integration for AI systems, and lifecycle performance consistency monitoring.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI governance framework components, including AI system registries, tiered review processes, impact assessment methodologies, and post-deployment monitoring requirements.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 15(1) (obligation) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-006 is the strongest match (STS: 0.558, cosine: 0.648). Secondary: AI-T-007 (AI, cosine: 0.643), AG-K-004 (AG, cosine: 0.635).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.6/10 (raw 0.558). Shared vocabulary: [ai, knowledge, monitoring, system]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [accuracy, assurance, benchmarking, consistency, cybersecurity, engineering] vs WIDAI terms [assessment, components, deployment, framework, governance, impact] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.892 (P=0.894, R=0.890). Balanced P/R — the 25-word competency implication and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (95.6%). Semantic tension between "Knowledge of AI system quality engineering, including accuracy measurement and b" and "Knowledge of AI governance framework components, including AI system registries,". Despite STS-based 'Superset of' classification, these concepts may operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.648 > STS 0.558 (gap +0.090). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, knowledge, monitoring, system] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 20.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, knowledge, monitoring, system]. EU AI Act-only: [accuracy, assurance, benchmarking, consistency, cybersecurity, engineering]. WIDAI-only: [assessment, components, deployment, framework, governance, impact].
